commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add Google Sheets transitionary client tracker

Adds tooling to bridge the gap between the cancelled client success
tracker and the full CST app, allowing two coaches on separate networks
to log notes and track client progress in real time during the transition.

scripts/generate-sheets-csv.py
- Reads Client-data-updated.xlsx and outputs two CSVs to sheets-export/
- clients.csv: one row per client (260 clients) with Name, Email, Offer,
  Coach, Next Step, Last Contact Date
- notes-log.csv: all historical notes (2,890 entries) including current
  next step notes merged in from the Current Data sheet, sorted A→Z by
  client then oldest→newest within each client

scripts/apps-script.js
- Paste-and-go Google Apps Script for the imported Sheet
- "CST Tools" menu with two items:
  - Add Today's Entry: modal dialog with client selector, pre-filled date,
    coach field (remembered between sessions), and notes textarea;
    appends to Notes Log and atomically updates Clients Col E + Col F
  - View Client History: modal showing all notes for a selected client,
    newest first, with date formatted as "Month D, YYYY"
- Data integrity fixes: submit button disables on click to prevent
  double-submit; Clients sheet updated in a single setValues call;
  SpreadsheetApp.flush() called before returning to force writes to disk

scripts/SHEETS-SETUP.md
- Step-by-step setup guide: generate CSVs, import into Google Sheets,
  format sheets, install Apps Script, share with second user, day-to-day
  usage instructions, and export process for future CST import

.gitignore
- Added exclusions for Client-data*.xlsx, Client-data*.json,
  sheets-export/, and nul to keep sensitive client data out of the repo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

directory with five chapters on governance, succession, and obligations.

three-framework synthesis explicit (Parfit/Scanlon/genealogical);
documentation step reframed as delivering the invitation.

commit.message:
feat: chat UI components, user profiles, source precedence, RAG enrichment

Sprint 4 polish + Sprint 5 foundation:

A — Chat UI: Extract 925-line monolith into dedicated components
  - Council components: VerdictChip, Scoreboard, AgentCard, ConflictMatrix,
    DebateTimeline, CouncilResultPanel, parseCouncilData
  - CSS modules with proper animations, ARIA, focus states, reduced-motion
  - Suspense boundary for useSearchParams (Next.js best practice)
  - Types imported from @q3/shared-contracts (SSOT, no inline duplicates)

B — User context profiles: Full CRUD
  - UserContextService (get/upsert with tenant scoping)
  - UserContextController (GET/PUT /user-context)
  - UserContextModule registered in AppModule
  - useUserContext + useUpdateUserContext hooks
  - apiClient.put() method added

D — RAG enrichment for council packet
  - AssetAnalysisPacket gains rag_context field
  - enrich_packet_with_rag() retrieves ticker-specific context
  - Packet serializes ragContext in to_dict()

E — Response builder with source precedence
  - SourceType enum: structured_internal > internal_rag > external_web > model_prior
  - Citation + SourceBlock + BuiltResponse types
  - Divergence detection when web contradicts internal data
  - Source/citation Zod schemas in shared-contracts (SSOT)
  - 5 tests covering precedence, divergence, empty blocks

commit.message:
Add base ring (single & double) to externals module

New external component types:
- Base Ring (Single): annular plate welded to skirt bottom
- Base Ring (Double): two rings with vertical stiffener plates

Includes full support across all layers:
- types.ts: ExternalType union, ExternalItem fields, weight calc,
  weld length calc, createNewExternal defaults
- defaults.ts: sub-assembly preset for double base ring
- ExternalsModule.tsx: editor form with ring OD/ID/thickness,
  stiffener config section, dimension tracking, weld auto-derive
- External3DViewer.tsx: 3D models with skirt cylinder, annular
  ring geometry, stiffener plates, orange/green weld indicators
- ExternalDiagram.tsx: 2D SVG section diagrams with hatched
  sections, fillet weld symbols, dimension annotations

feat: add Copilot premium request tracking and daily limit (#144) (#145)

worker types (Smith, Warden, Schematic, CI fix, review fix, rebase).
Each invocation counts as 1 request × model multiplier (e.g. opus 4.6
= 3x, haiku 4.5 = 0.33x, free models = 0x).

Changes:
- Add CopilotPremiumMultiplier() lookup table in internal/cost/
- Add copilot_premium_requests table in state.db (date, requests_used)
- Add copilot_daily_request_limit config setting (int, default 0)
- Record premium requests after each copilot invocation in all workers
- Filter copilot from provider chain when daily limit exceeded
- Expose copilot request stats in IPC status payload
- Add UsedProvider field to warden.ReviewResult for tracking

commit.message:
fix: robust solver cascade and missing internal functions

alignment.py:
- Add concentration detection: returns uniform weights when kernels are
  near-constant (exponential concentration), instead of crashing the solver
- Multi-solver cascade: CLARABEL → ECOS → SCS → closed-form fallback
- Use cp.psd_wrap() to avoid CVXPY recomputing PSD decomposition
- Adaptive Tikhonov regularization scaled by matrix Frobenius norm
- Catch all solver exceptions (not just ImportError)

statistical_analysis.py:
- Add _frobenius_alignment() — was imported by ablation.py but missing

visualization.py:
- Add _kernel_off_diagonal_stats() — was imported by ablation.py but missing

commit.message:
Fix sparkles: replace SVG with CSS-only to prevent giant render before CSS loads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add classic chart patterns: H&S, Double Top/Bottom, Cup & Handle

5 new patterns detected from candle math (no chart image needed):
1. Head & Shoulders — 3 swing highs, middle highest, sides equal (sellScore +3)
2. Inverse Head & Shoulders — 3 swing lows, middle lowest (buyScore +3)
3. Double Bottom (W) — 2 lows at same level with bounce between (buyScore +3)
4. Double Top (M) — 2 highs at same level with dip between (sellScore +3)
5. Cup & Handle — U-shape cup + shallow pullback handle (buyScore +3)

All detected using swing point math on 30-bar window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit — Heritage Motor multi-tenant vehicle storage platform

Backend (Go 1.22+ / Fiber v2 / PostgreSQL 16):
- REST API with 30+ endpoints (auth, vehicles, bays, events, tasks, documents, users, audit)
- JWT auth (HS256) with refresh token rotation, TOTP MFA, bcrypt
- RBAC middleware (admin, operator, technician) with per-route enforcement
- PostgreSQL RLS for tenant isolation, 11 migrations, append-only events/audit
- S3-compatible document storage (Hetzner Object Storage)
- Rate limiting, CORS, request ID propagation, contextual logging
- Security audit complete: all CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW items resolved

Frontend (Next.js 14 PWA):
- Mobile-first field tool for technicians and operators
- QR code scanning with @zxing/browser, offline queue with IndexedDB
- Zustand state, SWR data fetching, role-based UI
- httpOnly cookie refresh token flow via Next.js API routes

Infrastructure:
- Docker Compose (postgres, api, pwa, caddy)
- Caddy reverse proxy with Cloudflare Origin Certificate TLS
- Per-host security headers, HTTP/3 support

Documentation:
- 7 technical docs (README, architecture, API reference, security, deployment, database, PWA)
- Accuracy-verified against source code (16 corrections applied)

First-time scrape: new contacts always scraped on next scheduler run

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix copilot provider model selection — honor Model field from config (Forge-6g6) (#143)

* feat: switch Copilot CLI output from PlainText to StreamJSON (JSONL) (#142)

Change Copilot provider to use --output-format json instead of --silent,
returning StreamJSON from Format(). This enables readStreamJSON to parse
Copilot's JSONL events for token counting and cost estimation.

commit.message:
fix: Corrección de detección de tarifa NR y A SOLICITUD en habitaciones expandidas

Bugs corregidos:
1. naturleon-scraper: al expandir habitaciones, el spread ...hotel propagaba noReembolsable=true
   y el detalles con texto 'NO REEMBOLSABLE' del primer room NR a TODAS las habitaciones.
   Fix: Override explícito de noReembolsable y detalles por habitación.

2. promo-finder detectarTipoTarifa: usaba JSON.stringify(incluye) para detectar 'A SOLICITUD',
   lo que causaba falsos positivos cuando amenidades como NaturCharter incluían
   texto 'sujeto a disponibilidad'. Fix: 'A SOLICITUD' solo se detecta desde promo/detalles
   (badges/ribbons de la habitación), no desde la lista de amenidades.

3. ficha-coti-processor _formatOption: isNonRefundable ahora usa isOptionNonRefundable()
   correctamente. 'A SOLICITUD' se muestra como 'Sujeto a confirmación de disponibilidad'
   en lugar del confuso 'Tarifa: A SOLICITUD'.

commit.message:
Add Shape Pattern Analysis — multi-candle visual pattern recognition

6 patterns detected from candle data (no chart needed):
1. Compression/Squeeze — range narrowing >30%, breakout imminent
2. Spring V-Shape — sharp drop + recovery in 7 bars
3. Rounding Bottom — U-shape accumulation over 20 bars
4. Ascending Support — 3+ consecutive higher swing lows
5. Descending Resistance — 3+ consecutive lower swing highs
6. Candle Strength — close position + body ratio analysis

Integrated into generateSignal scoring system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix WhatsApp: strip emojis from wa.me URL, fix price/bathroom formatting

commit.message:
RHIDP-12205: CQA 2.1 compliance for Audit logs in Red Hat Developer Hub (#1877)

* RHIDP-12205: CQA 2.1 compliance for Audit logs in Red Hat Developer Hub title

Apply CQA 2.1 standards for DITA 1.3 compatibility and AEM migration readiness:
- Add short descriptions with [role="_abstract"] to assembly and all modules
- Remove DITA-incompatible block title delimiters (--) from example blocks
- Convert callout list to description list format
- Apply one sentence per line formatting to substep descriptions
- Fix Red Hat style: change "using" to "by using"

Validation results: Vale DITA (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 suggestions) and Vale Red Hat style (0 errors, 0 warnings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

* RHIDP-12205: Apply code review suggestions and align IDs with titles

Apply all inline code review suggestions from lhite8041 and update module IDs
to follow CQA guidelines (no proc- prefix, match title exactly).

Changes to proc-audit-log-view.adoc:
- Updated ID: proc-audit-log-view → view-audit-logs-in-product-short
- Improved abstract: Split into two sentences for clarity
- Fixed Red Hat style: "using" → "by using" after noun

Changes to proc-forward-audit-log-splunk.adoc:
- Updated title: "Forwarding" → "Forward" (gerund to imperative)
- Updated ID: proc-forward-audit-log-splunk → forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk
- Combined abstract: Merged abstract and following sentence into single, complete sentence
- Improved AtLeastOnce description: More concise wording per review suggestion

All changes maintain Vale DITA compliance (0 errors, 0 warnings) and address
reviewer feedback for improved clarity and consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Convert audit log configuration from concept to procedure module

Convert con-audit-log-config.adoc from a concept module to a procedure module
following modular documentation templates.

Changes:
- Changed content type: CONCEPT → PROCEDURE
- Updated title: "Configuring audit logs" → "Configure audit logs" (gerund to imperative)
- Updated file name: con-audit-log-config.adoc → proc-configure-audit-logs.adoc
- Updated ID to follow CQA guidelines:
  - Old: con-audit-log-config_{context}
  - New: configure-audit-logs-for-product-short-on-ocp-short_{context}
- Restructured content to follow procedure template:
  - Added Prerequisites section
  - Converted description lists to numbered procedure steps with substeps
  - Moved Additional resources links into procedure steps for context
- Updated include statement in assembly-audit-log.adoc

The original content described configuration steps, which is procedural content,
so this conversion aligns the module type with its actual content.

Vale DITA validation: 0 errors, 0 warnings
Vale Red Hat style: 0 errors (4 URL warnings are false positives)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Align procedure module file names with titles in audit log

Renamed procedure modules to match their titles following CQA 2.1 naming conventions:
- proc-audit-log-view.adoc → proc-view-audit-logs-in-product-short.adoc
- proc-forward-audit-log-splunk.adoc → proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc

Updated include statements in assembly-audit-log.adoc to reference renamed files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Simplify abstract and use terminal source type for commands

Simplified the abstract in proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc from 217 to 113 characters by removing implementation details.

Changed all terminal commands from [source,bash] to [source,terminal] to follow the correct convention for terminal commands versus bash scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Remove passive voice from procedure

Converted passive voice to active voice in proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc to improve clarity and follow Red Hat style guidelines:
- "where the captured logs are sent" → "where to send the captured logs"
- "Tuning can be applied" → "You can apply tuning"
- "are re-sent" → "the forwarder re-sends"
- "logs are duplicated" → "forwarder might duplicate some logs"
- "logs are being forwarded" → "Splunk instance receives logs"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Update CQA.md with procedure writing guidelines

Added new sections to CQA.md based on learnings from audit log work:
- Source code block types: Use [source,terminal] for terminal commands, not [source,bash]
- Voice and tense in procedures: Avoid passive voice, use present tense
- Abstract guidelines: Keep concise (50-300 chars), focus on essential action

These guidelines help ensure procedures follow Red Hat style and DITA compliance requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

docs: Update architecture flows with recent code changes

Reflects commits from reciprocity_commit_analysis.csv:

ONBOARDING FLOW:
- Multi-vector dimension steering (6 dimensions for matching)
- Topic dwelling prevention (MAX_QUESTIONS_PER_TOPIC = 2)
- Hard topic blacklist constraint (pre/post LLM validation)
- Slot restoration from Supabase when Redis expires (BUG-029)
- Completion gate: requires ≥4/6 dimensions
- Progress formula: 70% multi-vector + 30% slots

COMPLETION WORKFLOW (REC-487):
- Background processing with ThreadPoolExecutor
- Sync (~3s): Profile creation, return immediately
- Background (~60s): Embeddings, matching, persona
- Retry logic: 60s → 120s → 240s with Sentry alerts

DASHBOARD FLOW (REC-487):
- "Finding matches..." state after onboarding
- Polling every 5s while matches generating
- Auto-clear after 2 min or when matches appear

MATCHING FLOW:
- Background execution via ThreadPoolExecutor
- Retry logic with exponential backoff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: V6 — action masking only, revert hyperparams to V4

V5 regressed to 10% success (V4 was 30%) due to changing too many
hyperparams at once. V6 keeps action masking (25D→13D) but reverts
gamma=0.8, gae_lambda=0.9, ent_coef=0.0, vf_coef=0.5. Trains for
100M steps to isolate action masking benefit.
